title: Something’s off with Midjourney’s pivot to body scanners
summary: Last week, Midjourney, an AI startup best known for its image generator, made an unusual pivot: medical imaging. The company announced a futuristic ultrasound scanner that would dunk users into a vat of water and, hopefully, produce "something as powerful as MRI" yet "as casual as a trip to the spa." Midjourney says the goal […]
claim: produce "something as powerful as an MRI" with a futuristic ultrasound scanner
myth_angle: Midjourney's claim of creating an ultrasound scanner that can produce MRI-quality images in 60 seconds may be an overstatement, and the actual capabilities and limitations of the technology need to be examined.
verify_reason: The snippets show that Midjourney's claim of producing something as powerful as an MRI with an ultrasound scanner has not been independently verified and the prototype's performance does not match the claimed 60 seconds, with one report stating it runs for 20 minutes.

title: How much would the Steam Machine cost to build?
summary: The Steam Machine is here, with a base price of $1,049. Yes, that's nearly twice the price of a PS5, but what you're buying here isn't a console but a full-on PC - and a tiny one at that. Valve says it's selling the Steam Machine basically at cost. So we asked ourselves: What would […]
claim: Valve says it's selling the Steam Machine basically at cost
myth_angle: Valve's 'at cost' pricing claim may not account for all expenses, such as research and development, marketing, and profit margins, which could be hidden in the final price of the Steam Machine.
verify_reason: The snippets show varying price predictions and estimates for the Steam Machine, with some sources suggesting it could be priced higher than the cost of its components, which contradicts Valve's claim of selling at cost.
